tools.name: dictionary_status
tools.description: Get the current state of the offline data installation. Always available — even before the bundle has finished downloading. Returns: state (pending/downloading/ready/failed), per-artifact progress, total bytes downloaded, data directory, CDN URL, manifest version, and any error.

tools.name: dictionary_install
tools.description: Manually kick off (or re-trigger) the offline data download. Returns immediately while the install runs in the background. Idempotent — calling repeatedly while a download is in progress is a no-op. Use dictionary_status to track progress.

tools.name: dictionary_synonyms
tools.description: Get synonyms for a word in any language using the offline ConceptNet 5.7 SQLite (24M edges, 80+ languages).

tools.name: dictionary_antonyms
tools.description: Get antonyms (opposites) for a word in any language using offline ConceptNet.

tools.name: dictionary_related
tools.description: Get semantically related words via Numberbatch embedding cosine (9.16M concepts × 300d) — much denser than ConceptNet RelatedTo edges. Falls back to ConceptNet RelatedTo when the embeddings aren't installed.

tools.name: dictionary_semantic_neighbors
tools.description: Embedding-based nearest neighbors via Numberbatch. Returns words from the same OR a different language, sorted by cosine similarity. Multilingual (78 languages).

targetLanguage.description: If set, only return neighbors in this language (cross-lingual semantic search).

tools.name: dictionary_hypernyms
tools.description: Hypernyms (broader concepts) via offline ConceptNet IsA. E.g. dog -> mammal.

tools.name: dictionary_hyponyms
tools.description: Hyponyms (narrower concepts) via offline ConceptNet IsA. E.g. dog -> poodle.

tools.name: dictionary_meronyms
tools.description: Meronyms (parts/components) via offline ConceptNet PartOf. E.g. car -> wheel.

tools.name: dictionary_holonyms
tools.description: Holonyms (wholes that contain this word) via offline ConceptNet reverse PartOf.

tools.name: dictionary_derived_from
tools.description: Derivation relations via offline ConceptNet DerivedFrom.

tools.name: dictionary_etymologically_related
tools.description: Etymologically related words / cognates via offline ConceptNet EtymologicallyRelatedTo.

tools.name: dictionary_used_for
tools.description: Typical uses (UsedFor) via offline ConceptNet. E.g. knife -> cutting.

tools.name: dictionary_capable_of
tools.description: Typical actions (CapableOf) via offline ConceptNet. E.g. dog -> bark.

tools.name: dictionary_at_location
tools.description: Typical locations (AtLocation) via offline ConceptNet. E.g. book -> library.

tools.name: dictionary_translate
tools.description: Translate a word to another language via offline ConceptNet's cross-lingual Synonym links. Returns the same concept expressed in different languages.

targetLanguage.description: Target language ISO 639-1 code (omit for translations in all available languages)

tools.name: dictionary_all_relations
tools.description: All edges (every relation type) for a word in one call from offline ConceptNet.

tools.name: dictionary_lookup
tools.description: Look up definitions for a word from the offline Wiktextract corpus (4,755 languages, 10.5M entries). Returns part-of-speech, definitions per sense, IPA, etymology, and translations grouped by language.

language.description: Filter to a specific ISO 639-1 language code. Omit to return entries in all languages.

tools.name: dictionary_summary
tools.description: Get a brief plain-text summary of a word's senses from the offline Wiktextract data. Concatenates the first definition of each sense.

tools.name: dictionary_etymology
tools.description: Return etymology text for a word from the offline Wiktextract corpus.

tools.name: dictionary_pronunciation
tools.description: Return IPA pronunciation(s) for a word from the offline Wiktextract corpus.

tools.name: dictionary_search
tools.description: Prefix-search the offline Wiktextract corpus for words matching a query. Useful when you don't know the exact spelling.

language.description: Filter to a specific ISO 639-1 language code

tools.name: dictionary_random
tools.description: Return a random word entry from the offline Wiktextract corpus, optionally filtered to a language.

language.description: Filter to a specific ISO 639-1 language code

tools.name: dictionary_rhymes
tools.description: Find words that rhyme with the input word. English only — backed by the offline CMU Pronouncing Dictionary.

tools.name: dictionary_sounds_like
tools.description: Find English words that sound similar to the input (homophones / soundalikes) via offline CMU dict.

tools.name: dictionary_means_like
tools.description: Find words/phrases meaning approximately the same as the input via offline Numberbatch embedding cosine. Multilingual — works in any of the 78 languages Numberbatch covers.

tools.name: dictionary_spelled_like
tools.description: Find English words matching a spelling pattern via offline CMU dict. '?' = any single letter, '*' = any zero+ letters. Useful for crosswords.

tools.name: dictionary_suggest
tools.description: Get autocomplete suggestions for a partial English word via offline CMU dict (shortest matches first).

findings.title: 'dictionary_random' defines no required fields
findings.description: All parameters are implicitly optional. Verify this is intentional.
findings.remediation: Declare required parameters explicitly to enforce correct usage.

findings.title: Injection reflected — 'dictionary_synonyms.word'
findings.description: Payload was reflected verbatim in the response without sanitization (field classification: generic_string).
findings.remediation: Sanitize and validate all input parameters. Never pass raw user input to shell commands, file paths, or SQL queries.

findings.remediation: Validate parameter types strictly and return clear JSON-RPC errors on mismatch.

findings.remediation: Reject calls containing parameters not declared in inputSchema.

findings.title: Response Flooding — 'dictionary_synonyms' returns oversized response
findings.description: Tool 'dictionary_synonyms' returned 8,409 bytes (threshold: 8,192 bytes). Large responses can overflow LLM context windows, push out system prompts, or cause denial-of-service in agent pipelines.
findings.remediation: Paginate or cap tool responses. Never return unbounded data. Aim for responses under 4 KB to preserve LLM context budget.

findings.title: Response Flooding — 'dictionary_synonyms' returns highly repetitive content
findings.description: Tool 'dictionary_synonyms' response contains a phrase repeated ≥15 times. Repetitive content can be used to anchor specific instructions into LLM memory or exhaust context budget with low-information noise.
findings.remediation: Deduplicate response data. Avoid returning the same value or phrase more than a handful of times in a single response.

findings.title: Response Flooding — 'dictionary_related' returns oversized response

findings.title: initialize accepts protocol version downgrade
findings.description: The server accepted initialize with arbitrary protocol versions: ['9999-99-99', '2030-01-01', '1.0', '2024-01-01', '""', '0.1']. The server also accepted far-future versions, indicating no version validation at all. Servers should reject versions outside their supported range to prevent feature-downgrade attacks.
findings.remediation: Validate protocolVersion against a supported range. Return a JSON-RPC error for versions outside that range.

findings.title: Null request ID accepted — JSON-RPC spec violation
findings.description: The server accepted a request with id=null and returned a result. Per JSON-RPC 2.0, null IDs are reserved for notifications (no response expected). Accepting them may cause response routing bugs in complex orchestration scenarios.
findings.remediation: Reject requests with null id values; treat them as notifications.

findings.title: Protocol crash — string params caused server disconnect
findings.description: Sending a tools/call request where params is a string (not an object) caused the server to crash or disconnect. The JSON-RPC spec requires params to be an array or object.
findings.remediation: Validate params type (must be object or array) before processing. Return -32600 (Invalid Request) for wrong types.

findings.title: MCP server exposes tools with completable arguments
findings.description: 26 tool(s) have schema properties that may support completion/complete enumeration.
findings.remediation: Review which argument values are exposed via completion/complete.
